_comment: Distilled from Diagnostic Cystoscopy_Medco.pdf and Diagnostic Cystoscopy_PPD.CPD.pdf. HBP 2.0 code.
clinical_indications: To look at the lining of the bladder and the urethra.
Evaluation of patients with voiding symptoms, urologic fistulas, urethral or bladder diverticula, Congenital anomalies' in pediatrics

Intravesical procedures (e.g., for treatment of bladder stones, bladder ulcers, or bladder tumors; removal of foreign bodies in the bladder; botulinum toxin injection; and ureteral catheterization in association with some gynecologic problems)

preauth.label: Detailed Clinical notes with history, indication for procedure, symptoms, signs, examination findings and advice for admission

ppd_preauth.q: Are detailed Clinical notes – all vitals, detailed history especially previous surgery, symptoms, signs, physical examination including local examination, indication for procedure, advise for admission, and planned line of treatment available?

cpd_claim.q: Are the detailed ICPs with daily vitals and treatment details available?
Are the detailed procedure / Operative Notes available?
Is the Discharge summary with follow-up advise at the time of discharge?

common_queries: Clinical notes and/or imaging report (Xray/USG/CT Scan/MRI) not indicative of procedure.
Detailed clinical notes for pre-authorization missing or incomplete.
Xray/USG/CT Scan/MRI report missing at pre-authorization.
Detailed Indoor Case Papers (ICPs) missing or incomplete at claim submission.
Detailed Procedure / Operative Notes missing at claim submission.
Discharge summary with follow-up advice missing at claim submission.

clinical_key_pointers: Cystoscopy is a visual inspection of the inside bladder and urethra.
Cystoscopy can be performed in several settings, including OPD or an operating room, with either topical pain medicine (a numbing jelly in the urethra) or under an aesthetic (general or sedation).
